blocks.content: Orkamph raised his hand to welcome them.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: You must have had a hard journey coming such a long way. Shirone, you know the reason you've come here, don't you?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, I know.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Good, we'll discuss the details later in a separate place. You must be weary from the journey, so prepare rooms for them and offer them a meal.
The head chamberlain, who oversaw all affairs in the royal palace, bowed his head.

blocks.speaker: Head Chamberlain
blocks.content: As you wish, Your Majesty.
Shirone once again lifted his head to look at Orkamp.
It was a reunion between father and son after 18 years. However, there were no personal words exchanged.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Was this what it meant to be a king? Did having the title of king mean that one couldn't even express personal emotions?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Perhaps he had no feelings left for the son he had already abandoned.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If he truly loved his child, he wouldn't have abandoned him, no matter what kind of obstacles he faced.
With a sense of emptiness, Shirone and his group followed the chamberlain out of the grand hall.
The Great Chamber where guests were accommodated had rooms arranged for two people each.
Vincent and Olena shared a room, while Amy and Reina took another.
However, the chamberlain didn't assign a room to Sirone.

blocks.speaker: Chamberlain
blocks.content: Sir Shirone will be staying in the inner quarters with His Majesty's family.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: We weren't informed about this?
It was dangerous for Shirone to be separated from them, especially within a royal palace where any conspiracy could be lurking.

blocks.speaker: Chamberlain
blocks.content: But of course. Sir Shirone is to become royalty.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: But nothing is certain yet.
The chamberlain furrowed his brow.
Such matters were beyond the knowledge of a mere chamberlain.

blocks.speaker: Ordos
blocks.content: What seems to be the issue?
The royal administrative officer, Ordos, approached.
The chamberlain hastily bowed and conveyed the complaints of Shirone's party.

blocks.speaker: Ordos
blocks.content: There are protocols in the palace. Even simply changing a royal's room causes quite a stir. However, if you are that uncomfortable, I will make a petition.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Not wanting to cause any trouble right after entering the royal palace, Shirone shook his head.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: While it was true that he was wary of potential dangers, he still wanted to believe that his biological parents wouldn't harm him.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If that wasn't the case… If even they didn't see him as their son despite sharing the same blood, then there was no reason for him to be here.

blocks.speaker: Ordos
blocks.content: Understood. I'll go to the inner quarters. But I'll be able to see everyone at mealtime, right?

blocks.speaker: Ordos
blocks.content: Separate arrangements will be made for meals. A commoner cannot join the royal family's meal time.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone frowned.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Vincent and Olena were his benefactors who had raised him for 18 years and were his only parents.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even if he was of royal blood, he had no desire to deny that fact.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Come to think of it, no one from Kazra had yet expressed gratitude to Vincent and Olena.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Perhaps they believed they hadn't abandoned their child but had merely entrusted him temporarily.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Just as a vassal follows the king's command, it seemed they also considered Vincent and Olena's kindness as something to be taken for granted.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That was something Shirone could not tolerate.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If his parents were going to be treated with such disdain, he wouldn't hesitate to return home, even now.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's not acceptable. Please allow my parents to join me for the meal.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Sh- Shirone… we're fine.
Having spent his whole life tormented not by royalty but even by nobles, he knew just how dangerous Shirone's words were.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No. If that's the case, then I won't attend either. My parents must join me during meals.
Ordos bit his lip and fell into thought.
What an audacious child. Did he think he already had everything just because he was named the First Prince?
Of course, he would become a prince, but that didn't mean the power structure would change.
Within the royal palace, the current First Prince, Empress Terraje's son, still existed.
As his thoughts reached this point, Ordos found it all the more puzzling.
Despite the grand proclamation of bringing in Eliza's biological son, there had been no response or action from Terraje's side.
It could mean that she was confident, or perhaps it was an unspoken pressure on the forces she had planted within Kazra to act as they saw fit.
The Empress's power was formidable. Until now, there had been no way to challenge her, so factions hadn't clearly formed, but from now on, it was time to choose one's alliances carefully.
However, Ordos remained skeptical.
No matter how outstanding Sirone was at the magic academy, he was not an opponent that Terraje's children would be concerned about, not with just that level of reputation.

blocks.speaker: Ordos
blocks.content: I understand your request, Sir Shirone, but it's not within my authority to decide. This goes against royal protocol and could escalate into a national issue.

blocks.speaker: Ordos
blocks.content: Therefore, I must ask you to directly address His Majesty. Would that be acceptable?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Fine. I'll speak to him myself.

blocks.speaker: Ordos
blocks.content: Then please follow me. I will guide you to the inner quarters.
As Shirone followed Ordos, he turned back and said.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'll be back soon. See you later.
Reina watched Shirone as he grew distant.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Was he really planning to bring Vincent and Olena to dine at the royal table?
The etiquette of royalty supersedes even the nation's laws. In a monarchy, any insult to the king's authority could shake the very foundation of the country.
This decision was bound to invite danger in one way or another.

blocks.speaker: Olena
blocks.content: We're really fine. I think it would be better to stop Shirone. Even if I wanted to say something, I just… don't know enough.
It wasn't simply because Olena lacked knowledge.
Reina also couldn't quite grasp Shirone's intentions or what he was thinking.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: This is strange. He's not the type to be so stubborn.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Shirone has already started a fight.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: The reason Shirone responded to the palace's invitation was to meet his biological parents. But Shirone has no interest in becoming an heir or a prince.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: He intends to return to the magic academy and continue living with his family. And of course, that family is the two of you here.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: That's why he can't compromise. If he backs down here, he'll be pushed around to the very end.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Shirone chose the royal family's dining table as the point to gain a strategic advantage.
Olena's eyes welled up with tears.

blocks.speaker: Olena
blocks.content: They were such unworthy parents. In a situation like this, instead of protecting their son, they were the ones receiving his care.

blocks.speaker: Olena
blocks.content: In truth, she wanted to fight, too. Regardless of whether it was the king or anyone else, she wanted to stand up and declare that she had raised him, so he was her child.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Let's just watch. He's our son, after all. He's a thoughtful boy. We just need to follow what he says.
Olena wiped her tears and nodded.
Shirone entered the inner quarters where the royal family resided.
It was like another palace within the palace, a completely different sight from what he had seen so far.
The walls were adorned with portraits of the royal family, decorated with bright golden wallpaper, and luxurious carpets covered the floor.
Shirone made his way to Orkamp's room. Since it was located at the farthest end of the palace, it wasn't difficult to find.
When he entered the study, Orkamp was seated at his desk, lost in thought.
It was only after a considerable time that he realized Shirone was there, and he stood up, saying,

blocks.speaker: Orkamp
blocks.content: You've come?

blocks.speaker: Orkamp
blocks.content: Come this way. Your mother is waiting.
By 'mother,' he meant Queen Eliza of Kazra. Orkamp said no more and did not speak a single word as they walked.
Shirone was so tense that he didn't even register the silence.
He was finally about to meet the mother who had given birth to him.
Eliza's room was said to be the most lavish and luxurious place in the Kazra Kingdom.
A gigantic, pure white bed that could fit ten people was placed in the room, and sitting atop it was an even paler woman.
A queen didn't necessarily have to be beautiful, but she was, and her large eyes were filled with sorrow.

blocks.speaker: Eliza
blocks.content: Ah…
Overcome with emotion, Eliza couldn't speak.
Rising from the bed, she didn't say any of the words she had prepared beforehand but instead embraced Shirone.
Shirone just stood there. He felt that he should hug her back, but for some reason, he couldn't move even a finger.
She had a pleasant fragrance. It was an exquisite and precious royal scent that Shirone had never experienced before.
But it wasn't Olena's scent.

blocks.speaker: Eliza
blocks.content: My son, you've finally come. I'm so sorry…! Please forgive your mother!
Hearing Eliza's tearful voice, Shirone felt his throat tighten as well.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: When he was younger, there were times he resented the parents who had abandoned him.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But Vincent and Olena had filled the emptiness of his bloodline with a love so immense that it could never be replaced.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's why he felt guilty. It was unfamiliar to him that he couldn't say kind words to Eliza.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Outside… the parents who raised me are waiting.
Eliza pulled back and examined Shirone's face.
He was a truly beautiful boy. This too was of noble blood, both his own and Orkamp's.

blocks.speaker: Eliza
blocks.content: Yes, but not anymore. I am your mother now. You will become the First Prince and inherit the kingdom.

blocks.speaker: Eliza
blocks.content: You have nothing to worry about. From now on, I will protect you.
Shirone felt a tightness in his chest.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He didn't yet understand the emotion that was making him angry, but for the first time, he regretted coming here.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I want to have a meal with them. I understand that it goes against the laws of the kingdom, but I believe they deserve that kind of respect.
Eliza looked at her husband with a puzzled expression.
Orkamp was also disappointed by Shirone's statement. Why would a mountaineer caretaker be important to someone who would become the king of a kingdom in the future?
Orkamp began to doubt whether Shirone had the qualities of an heir, but he soon shook his head.
If he had Orkamp's blood, there was no way he could lack those qualities.
It was simply that Shirone had not yet awakened the temperament of a conqueror because of the different environment he had grown up in.

blocks.speaker: Orkamp
blocks.content: Is this truly necessary? Meals are meant to be shared with family. I don't like the idea of uninvited guests intruding.

blocks.speaker: Orkamp
blocks.content: I will make sure they're treated well separately, so they won't feel neglected.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So, this was how it was? Did the laws of a single meal outweigh the effort of those who had raised him?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Was this why he had been discarded like a worn-out shoe when the kingdom faced a crisis?
Seeing the disappointment and frustration in Shirone's eyes, Eliza quickly changed her mind.
Even though she had agreed to abandon him back then, it had been entirely for her husband Orkamp's sake.
What mother would willingly abandon her child?
She had suffered a lifetime of guilt, and now that Shirone had returned, she was ready to grant whatever he desired.

blocks.speaker: Eliza
blocks.content: If that's what you wish, then so be it. They took care of you, so it's the least we can do.

blocks.speaker: Eliza
blocks.content: Of course, if that's what my son wants.
Moved by her own words, Eliza's emotions overwhelmed her again, and she pulled Shirone into her arms.
